name: Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus
description: Chief temple of Rome on the Capitoline Hill, dedicated to the Capitoline Triad (Jupiter, Juno, Minerva). Rebuilt multiple times after fires.

name: Temple of Saturn
description: One of the oldest temples in the Roman Forum, also served as the state treasury (aerarium).

name: Temple of Mars Ultor
description: Centrepiece of the Forum of Augustus, vowed by Augustus before the Battle of Philippi to avenge Caesar's assassination.

name: Temple of Venus and Roma
description: Largest temple in ancient Rome, designed by Emperor Hadrian, dedicated to Venus Felix and Roma Aeterna.

name: Temple of Vesta
description: Circular temple in the Roman Forum housing the sacred flame tended by the Vestal Virgins.

name: Temple of Castor and Pollux
description: Temple in the Roman Forum commemorating the divine twins' aid at the Battle of Lake Regillus.

name: Temple of Concordia
description: Temple at the western end of the Roman Forum dedicated to the goddess of harmony and agreement.

name: Pantheon
description: Built by Hadrian c. 126 AD with the largest unreinforced concrete dome in the world. Converted to a church in 609.

name: Temple of Divus Julius
description: Temple erected by Augustus on the spot where Julius Caesar was cremated in the Roman Forum.

name: Temple of Antoninus and Faustina
description: Well-preserved temple in the Roman Forum dedicated by the Senate to the deified Emperor Antoninus Pius and his wife Faustina.

name: Temple of Portunus
description: Small Ionic temple near the ancient river port of Rome, dedicated to the god of harbours.

name: Temple of Hercules Victor
description: Round temple in the Forum Boarium, the oldest surviving marble building in Rome.

name: Temple of Apollo Sosianus
description: Temple near the Theatre of Marcellus, rebuilt by C. Sosius in the Augustan period.

name: Temple of Minerva (Forum of Nerva)
description: Dominating temple of the Forum Transitorium, completed under Nerva.

name: Temple of Peace
description: Built by Vespasian after the Jewish War, housed spoils from the Temple of Jerusalem.

name: Temple of Magna Mater
description: Temple on the Palatine Hill dedicated to the Phrygian mother goddess Cybele.

name: Temple of Juno Moneta
description: Temple on the Arx of the Capitoline Hill, later associated with the Roman mint.

name: Temple of Divus Claudius
description: Massive temple platform on the Caelian Hill dedicated to the deified Emperor Claudius.

name: Temple of Hadrian
description: Temple in the Campus Martius dedicated to the deified Hadrian by his successor Antoninus Pius.

name: Temple of Jupiter Stator
description: Temple on the Via Sacra vowed during battles against the Sabines.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Baalbek)
description: Colossal temple complex in Heliopolis (Baalbek), one of the largest Roman religious structures ever built.

name: Temple of Bacchus (Baalbek)
description: Best-preserved of the Baalbek temples, remarkable for its ornate interior carvings.

name: Temple of Venus (Baalbek)
description: Circular temple with a unique concave entablature at the Baalbek complex.

name: Temple of Artemis (Ephesus)
description: One of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, destroyed and rebuilt multiple times.

name: Temple of Apollo (Delphi)
description: Home of the famous Oracle, the most important religious site in ancient Greece.

name: Temple of Apollo (Didyma)
description: Massive oracle temple in Ionia, one of the largest Greek temples ever planned.

name: Temple of Apollo (Delos)
description: Sacred birthplace of Apollo, major Panhellenic sanctuary and treasury.

name: Temple of Zeus (Olympia)
description: Housing Phidias' chryselephantine statue of Zeus, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

name: Temple of Olympian Zeus (Athens)
description: Colossal temple completed by Hadrian in 131 AD after 600 years of construction.

name: Temple of Hera (Paestum)
description: Two well-preserved Doric temples at the Greek colony of Poseidonia.

name: Temple of Hera (Olympia)
description: One of the oldest monumental temples in Greece, where the Olympic flame is lit today.

name: Temple of Neptune (Paestum)
description: Best-preserved Greek Doric temple, actually likely dedicated to Hera or Zeus.

name: Maison Carree
description: Exceptionally well-preserved Roman temple in Nemausus (Nimes), Gaul.

name: Temple of Augustus (Pula)
description: Well-preserved Augustan temple in the forum of Pola (Pula), Istria.

name: Temple of Augustus (Barcelona)
description: Four surviving Corinthian columns of the imperial cult temple in Barcino.

name: Temple of Augustus and Rome (Ankara)
description: Temple preserving the Res Gestae Divi Augusti inscription (Monumentum Ancyranum).

name: Parthenon
description: Temple of Athena Parthenos on the Athenian Acropolis, masterpiece of Classical architecture.

name: Erechtheion
description: Sacred temple on the Acropolis with the famous Porch of the Caryatids.

name: Temple of Athena Nike
description: Small Ionic temple on the bastion of the Acropolis celebrating military victories.

name: Temple of Hephaestus
description: Best-preserved ancient Greek temple, overlooking the Athenian Agora.

name: Temple of Poseidon (Sounion)
description: Dramatic cliff-top temple at Cape Sounion, landmark for ancient sailors.

name: Temple of Apollo Epicurius (Bassae)
description: Remote mountain temple in Arcadia, UNESCO World Heritage Site, designed by Iktinos.

name: Temple of Aphrodite (Aphrodisias)
description: Cult centre of Aphrodite in Caria, later converted to a Christian basilica.

name: Temple of Trajan (Pergamon)
description: Temple of the imperial cult crowning the acropolis of Pergamon.

name: Capitol (Dougga)
description: Well-preserved Capitoline temple at Thugga in Roman North Africa.

name: Capitol (Sbeitla)
description: Three separate temples to the Capitoline Triad at Sufetula, Tunisia.

name: Capitol (Volubilis)
description: Capitoline temple in the westernmost major Roman city in Mauretania Tingitana.

name: Temple of Janus (Autun)
description: Gallo-Roman tower-temple (fanum) at Augustodunum, one of best-preserved in Gaul.

name: Temple of Sulis Minerva (Bath)
description: Romano-British temple at Aquae Sulis combining native and Roman worship at the sacred hot springs.

name: Temple of Claudius (Colchester)
description: Major imperial cult temple in Camulodunum, the first Roman capital of Britain.

name: Temple of Mercury (Puy de Dome)
description: Mountain-top Gallo-Roman temple at the summit of Puy de Dome in Auvergne.

name: Temple of Diana (Evora)
description: Roman temple in Liberalitas Julia (Evora), one of the best-preserved on the Iberian Peninsula.

name: Temple of Diana (Merida)
description: Forum temple of Augusta Emerita, capital of Lusitania.

name: Temple of Bel (Palmyra)
description: Syncretic temple combining Mesopotamian and Graeco-Roman elements at Palmyra.

name: Temple of Baalshamin (Palmyra)
description: Phoenician-Roman temple dedicated to the lord of the heavens at Palmyra.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Damascus)
description: Massive temple complex later converted to the Umayyad Mosque, originally a sanctuary of Hadad.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Split)
description: Small temple in Diocletian's Palace at Spalatum.

name: Temple of Augustus (Vienne)
description: Well-preserved Roman temple in Colonia Julia Vienna, southern Gaul.

name: Ara Pacis
description: Altar of Augustan Peace on the Campus Martius, masterpiece of Roman sculptural art.

name: Temple of Saturn (Dougga)
description: Temple dedicated to Saturn/Baal Hammon at Thugga, reflecting African religious syncretism.

name: Temple of Caelestis (Dougga)
description: Semi-circular temple dedicated to Juno Caelestis/Tanit at Thugga.

name: Capitolium (Ostia)
description: Main temple of Ostia Antica, dominating the forum of Rome's principal port city.

name: Temple of Roma and Augustus (Ostia)
description: Small temple on the south side of the forum at Ostia, for the imperial cult.

name: Temple of Garni
description: Only standing Graeco-Roman colonnaded temple in the former Soviet Union, in Armenia.

name: Temple of Apollo (Corinth)
description: Archaic Doric temple, one of the earliest stone temples in Greece.

name: Temple of Apollo (Pompeii)
description: Major temple in the forum of Pompeii, destroyed by the eruption of Vesuvius.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Pompeii)
description: Capitolium temple dominating the north end of the forum at Pompeii.

name: Temple of Venus (Pompeii)
description: Temple to the patron goddess of the Roman colony of Pompeii.

name: Capitolium (Brescia)
description: Well-preserved Capitoline temple at Brixia, now housing the Santa Giulia museum.

name: Temple of Hercules (Cori)
description: Doric temple on the acropolis of Cora in Latium.

name: Temple of Demeter (Eleusis)
description: Telesterion, hall of the Eleusinian Mysteries, the most famous secret rites of antiquity.

name: Sanctuary of Fortuna Primigenia (Palestrina)
description: Monumental terraced sanctuary with an oracle, architectural masterpiece of the late Republic.

name: Sanctuary of Hercules Victor (Tivoli)
description: Grand suburban sanctuary at Tibur rivalling Palestrina in scale.

name: Sanctuary of Jupiter Anxur (Terracina)
description: Dramatic cliff-top sanctuary overlooking the Via Appia at Tarracina.

name: Sanctuary of Diana (Nemi)
description: Sacred grove and temple on Lake Nemi, famous from Frazer's The Golden Bough.

name: Temple of Ceres (Paestum)
description: Archaic temple at Poseidonia, misnamed Temple of Ceres, actually dedicated to Athena.

name: Temple of Concord (Agrigento)
description: One of the best-preserved Greek temples, in the Valley of the Temples, Sicily.

name: Temple of Juno Lacinia (Agrigento)
description: Prominent Doric temple on the ridge at Akragas, overlooking the sea.

name: Temple of Segesta
description: Unfinished but well-preserved Doric temple of the Elymians in western Sicily.

name: Temple of Apollo (Syracuse)
description: One of the most ancient Doric temples in Sicily, in the Ortygia island.

name: Mithraeum of San Clemente
description: Underground mithraeum beneath the Basilica of San Clemente, excellently preserved with altar.

name: Mithraeum of the Circus Maximus
description: Mithraeum discovered near the Circus Maximus with well-preserved tauroctony fresco.

name: Mithraeum of the Baths of Caracalla
description: Large mithraeum in the substructures of the Baths of Caracalla.

name: Barberini Mithraeum
description: Mithraeum beneath Palazzo Barberini with painted vault decorations.

name: Mithraeum of Santa Prisca
description: Well-decorated mithraeum on the Aventine Hill with painted walls and inscriptions.

name: Mithraeum of the Seven Spheres (Ostia)
description: Mithraeum at Ostia with floor mosaics showing the seven planetary grades of initiation.

name: Mithraeum of Felicissimus (Ostia)
description: Mithraeum at Ostia with black-and-white mosaic floor depicting symbols of the seven grades.

name: Mithraeum of the Painted Walls (Ostia)
description: Mithraeum at Ostia notable for its painted wall decorations.

name: Mithraeum of the Animals (Ostia)
description: Mithraeum at Ostia with a marble group of Mithras slaying the bull.

name: Mithraeum of the Serpents (Ostia)
description: Small mithraeum at Ostia near the Porta Laurentina.

name: Mithraeum of Terme del Mitra (Ostia)
description: Mithraeum in the baths complex at Ostia with a marble tauroctony.

name: Carrawburgh Mithraeum
description: Well-excavated mithraeum at Brocolitia fort on Hadrian's Wall.

name: London Mithraeum
description: Mithraeum discovered in 1954 at Walbrook, reconstructed and open to visitors.

name: Mithraeum (Dura-Europos)
description: Frontier mithraeum with painted hunting scenes at the Euphrates garrison town.

name: Mithraeum of Capua
description: Well-preserved mithraeum near the amphitheatre of ancient Capua with painted frescoes.

name: Mithraeum of Aquileia
description: Mithraeum at the major military and commercial centre of northeastern Italy.

name: Mithraeum of Ptuj
description: Multiple mithraea at Poetovio, one of the richest Mithraic sites in the empire.

name: Mithraeum II of Ptuj
description: Second mithraeum at Poetovio with numerous votive reliefs.

name: Mithraeum III of Ptuj
description: Third mithraeum at Poetovio along the Drava river.

name: Mithraeum of Carnuntum
description: Mithraeum at the legionary base on the Danube frontier near modern Vienna.

name: Mithraeum II of Carnuntum
description: Second mithraeum in the civilian town of Carnuntum.

name: Mithraeum III of Carnuntum
description: Third mithraeum at Carnuntum, rebuilt in open-air museum.

name: Mithraeum of Tienen
description: Mithraeum in Roman Belgica with evidence of ritual feasting.

name: Mithraeum of Bordeaux
description: Mithraeum discovered at Burdigala, major city of Aquitania.

name: Mithraeum of Saarbrucken
description: Mithraeum near the Roman vicus on the Rhine-Danube frontier.

name: Mithraeum of Riegel
description: Mithraeum in the Upper Rhine frontier zone.

name: Mithraeum of Dieburg
description: Mithraeum in the Roman settlement near the Limes Germanicus.

name: Mithraeum of Stockstadt
description: Mithraeum at the fort on the Main-Limes frontier.

name: Mithraeum of Housesteads
description: Mithraeum near Vercovicium fort on Hadrian's Wall.

name: Mithraeum of Rudchester
description: Mithraeum at Vindobala fort on Hadrian's Wall.

name: Mithraeum of Inveresk
description: Mithraeum at a Roman fort near Edinburgh, northernmost known example.

name: Mithraeum of Caesarea Maritima
description: Mithraeum at the Roman provincial capital of Judaea.

name: Mithraeum of Sidon
description: Mithraeum at the Phoenician port city with a notable marble tauroctony.

name: Mithraeum of Nida-Heddernheim
description: Two mithraea at the Roman civitas capital near modern Frankfurt.

name: Mithraeum of Marino
description: Mithraeum near the Alban Hills south of Rome with a well-preserved fresco.

name: Mithraeum of Fertőrákos
description: Rock-cut mithraeum in Pannonia near the amber route.

name: Mithraeum of Doliche
description: Mithraeum near the cult centre of Jupiter Dolichenus in Commagene.

name: Mithraeum of Rožanec (Praetorium Latobicorum)
description: Mithraeum at a Roman road station in the province of Pannonia Superior.

name: Mithraeum of Bourg-Saint-Andeol
description: Rock-cut mithraeum relief in the Rhone valley, southern Gaul.

name: Mithraeum of Jajce
description: Rock-cut mithraeum in the Vrbas valley, Roman Dalmatia.

name: Church of the Holy Sepulchre
description: Built by Constantine over the traditional site of Jesus' crucifixion and burial.

name: Church of the Nativity
description: Constantinian basilica over the traditional birthplace of Jesus in Bethlehem.

name: Old St. Peter's Basilica
description: Constantinian basilica built over the tomb of the apostle Peter on Vatican Hill.

name: Lateran Basilica
description: Cathedral of the Bishop of Rome, first major Christian basilica, given by Constantine.

name: Hagia Sophia
description: First church built by Constantius II; later rebuilt by Justinian (532-537) as the greatest church of Christendom.

name: Church of the Holy Apostles
description: Mausoleum-church of Constantine in Constantinople, later burial place of emperors.

name: Dura-Europos House Church
description: Earliest identified Christian house church with baptistery frescoes, destroyed by Sasanid siege.

name: Basilica of Saint Paul Outside the Walls
description: Theodosian basilica over the tomb of the apostle Paul on the Via Ostiense.

name: Santa Maria Maggiore
description: Papal basilica built under Pope Sixtus III with the finest early Christian mosaics in Rome.

name: San Lorenzo fuori le Mura
description: Constantinian basilica over the tomb of the deacon Lawrence.

name: Santa Sabina
description: Early basilica on the Aventine with original 5th-century wooden doors showing biblical scenes.

name: Santa Pudenziana
description: One of the oldest churches in Rome with a magnificent 4th-century apse mosaic.

name: Basilica of St. John (Ephesus)
description: Church built over the traditional tomb of St. John the Apostle at Ephesus.

name: Cathedral of Trier
description: Oldest cathedral in Germany, built by Constantine's mother Helena over a Roman palace.

name: Basilica of Aquileia
description: Early Christian basilica with remarkable 4th-century floor mosaics in northeastern Italy.

name: Basilica of Sant'Apollinare Nuovo (Ravenna)
description: Theodoric's palace chapel with processional mosaics, built just after the fall of the West.

name: Basilica of San Vitale (Ravenna)
description: Octagonal church with celebrated Justinianic mosaics of Emperor and Empress.

name: Church of the Virgin (Thessaloniki)
description: One of several early Christian churches at Thessalonica, important Pauline city.

name: Basilica of St. Demetrius (Thessaloniki)
description: Major early Christian basilica dedicated to the patron saint of Thessaloniki.

name: Church of Hagia Eirene (Constantinople)
description: One of the first churches in Constantinople, preceded Hagia Sophia.

name: Basilica of the Annunciation (Nazareth)
description: Early church over a cave venerated as the site of the Annunciation.

name: Church of the Multiplication (Tabgha)
description: Church with 5th-century mosaic floor depicting loaves and fishes.

name: Basilica of Hippo Regius
description: Cathedral of St. Augustine, one of the Fathers of the Church, at Hippo in Numidia.

name: Basilica Majorum (Carthage)
description: Major basilica at Carthage associated with the martyrdom of Perpetua and Felicitas.

name: Basilica of Damous el-Karita (Carthage)
description: Largest known ancient church in North Africa, at Carthage.

name: Church of St. Simeon Stylites (Qalaat Semaan)
description: Cruciform church built around the pillar of the famous Syrian ascetic near Antioch.

name: Basilica of Leptis Magna
description: Byzantine basilica built within the ruins of the Roman forum at Leptis Magna.

name: Early Church of Corinth
description: Site of the Pauline community at Corinth, one of the earliest Christian congregations in Greece.

name: Church of St. Mary (Ephesus)
description: Double church and site of the Council of Ephesus (431 AD).

name: House of Peter (Capernaum)
description: House church built over the traditional home of the apostle Peter, with successive octagonal churches.

name: Church of Santa Costanza (Rome)
description: Circular mausoleum-church of Constantine's daughter, with outstanding 4th-century mosaics.

name: Basilica of St. Felix (Cimitile)
description: Pilgrimage basilica complex developed by Paulinus of Nola in Campania.

name: Basilica of St. Thecla (Milan)
description: One of the earliest cathedrals of Milan, preceding the Duomo.

name: Basilica of St. Ambrose (Milan)
description: Founded by Bishop Ambrose, major early Christian centre in northern Italy.

name: Cathedral of Barcelona
description: Early Christian basilica under the later medieval cathedral, attested from the Council of 343.

name: Basilica of Tarragona
description: Early Christian basilica at the provincial capital of Hispania Tarraconensis.

name: Old Cathedral of Braga
description: Earliest church at Bracara Augusta, capital of Gallaecia.

name: Basilica of St. Martin (Tours)
description: Pilgrimage church over the tomb of St. Martin, the apostle of Gaul.

name: Cathedral of Lyon
description: Site of one of the earliest Christian communities in Gaul, associated with the martyrs of 177 AD.

name: Early Church of London
description: Bishopric of Londinium attested at the Council of Arles (314 AD).

name: Temple of Isis (Pompeii)
description: Well-preserved temple of Isis in Pompeii, evidence of Egyptian cult penetration into Roman Italy.

name: Iseum Campense (Rome)
description: Major temple of Isis and Serapis on the Campus Martius, rebuilt by Domitian.

name: Temple of Isis (Delos)
description: Early Isis temple on the sacred island, evidence of Egyptian cults spreading through trade routes.

name: Temple of Isis (Philae)
description: Last functioning pagan temple in the Roman Empire, closed by Justinian. Relocated to Agilkia Island.

name: Serapeum of Alexandria
description: Great temple of Serapis in Alexandria, destroyed by Christians in 391 AD under Bishop Theophilus.

name: Serapeum (Rome, Quirinal)
description: Temple of Serapis on the Quirinal Hill, one of several in Rome.

name: Temple of Isis (Sabratha)
description: Isis temple at the Roman North African port city of Sabratha.

name: Iseum of Beneventum
description: Temple of Isis built by Domitian at Beneventum, featuring Egyptian obelisks and statues.

name: Temple of Isis (Londinium)
description: Evidence of Isis worship in Roman London from inscriptions and a jug inscription.

name: Temple of Isis (Mainz)
description: Isis and Magna Mater sanctuary at Mogontiacum, discovered in 1999.

name: Temple of Isis and Serapis (Gortyna)
description: Egyptian cult sanctuary at the capital of Roman Crete.

name: Serapeum of Ephesus
description: Monumental temple of Serapis near the commercial agora of Ephesus.

name: Temple of Isis (Cyrene)
description: Temple in the sanctuary area of Cyrene in North Africa.

name: Temple of Isis and Serapis (Thessaloniki)
description: Serapeum at Thessalonica, attested by inscriptions and archaeological finds.

name: Iseum of Ras el-Soda (Alexandria)
description: Suburban Isis sanctuary east of Alexandria.

name: Temple of Hathor (Dendera)
description: Late Ptolemaic-Roman temple, one of the best-preserved in Egypt, with famous zodiac ceiling.

name: Temple of Isis (Tivoli, Hadrian's Villa)
description: Serapeum-Canopus complex at Hadrian's Villa, reflecting imperial fascination with Egyptian cult.

name: Temple of Isis and Osiris (Abydos)
description: Ancient pilgrimage centre with Roman-era additions to the Osiris cult complex.

name: Temple of Horus (Edfu)
description: Best-preserved ancient Egyptian temple, completed in Ptolemaic-Roman era.

name: Temple of Khnum (Esna)
description: Roman-era temple hypostyle hall with the latest known hieroglyphic inscriptions (3rd c. AD).

name: Temple of Sobek and Haroeris (Kom Ombo)
description: Unusual double temple of the Ptolemaic-Roman period in Upper Egypt.

name: Temple of Mandulis (Kalabsha)
description: Largest free-standing temple of Egyptian Nubia, built in the Augustan period.

name: Serapeum of Memphis
description: Burial place of the Apis bulls, major pilgrimage centre near the pyramids of Saqqara.

name: Serapeum of Pergamon
description: Massive brick temple complex (Red Basilica) for Egyptian cults at Pergamon.

name: Temple of Isis (Stobi)
description: Egyptian cult sanctuary at the Macedonian city of Stobi.

name: Temple Mount (Second Temple)
description: Herod's rebuilding of the Second Temple, centre of Jewish worship until its destruction by Titus in 70 AD.

name: Synagogue of Ostia
description: One of the oldest synagogues in Europe, discovered near the ancient port of Rome.

name: Synagogue of Sardis
description: One of the largest ancient synagogues, occupying part of the Roman bath-gymnasium complex.

name: Synagogue of Dura-Europos
description: Famous for its extensive biblical wall paintings, preserved when the city was besieged.

name: Synagogue of Capernaum
description: White limestone synagogue in the Galilean village associated with Jesus' ministry.

name: Synagogue of Beit Alpha
description: 6th-century synagogue with famous zodiac mosaic floor in the Jezreel Valley.

name: Synagogue of Hammat Tiberias
description: Synagogue near the hot springs of Tiberias with zodiac mosaic floor.

name: Synagogue of Gamla
description: One of the oldest known synagogues, in the Golan Heights, destroyed during the Jewish Revolt.

name: Great Synagogue of Alexandria
description: Described in the Talmud as enormous, destroyed during the Kitos War (115-117 AD).

name: Synagogue of Rome (Trastevere)
description: Site of the ancient Jewish community in Trastevere, one of the oldest in Europe.

name: Synagogue of Priene
description: Converted house-synagogue in the Ionian Greek city of Priene.

name: Synagogue of Stobi
description: Well-documented synagogue at the Macedonian crossroads city.

name: Synagogue of Elche
description: Late Roman synagogue remains with mosaic floor in southeast Hispania.

name: Synagogue of Apamea
description: Synagogue with mosaic at the major city of Roman Syria.

name: Synagogue of Bova Marina
description: Southernmost ancient synagogue in Europe, in Calabria, with mosaic floor.

name: Oracle of Delphi
description: The Pythia, most famous oracle of the ancient world, consulted by Greek and Roman leaders for centuries.

name: Oracle of Dodona
description: Oldest Greek oracle, where priestesses interpreted the rustling of Zeus' sacred oak tree.

name: Oracle of Zeus Ammon (Siwa)
description: Desert oracle visited by Alexander the Great, blending Greek and Egyptian religion.

name: Asklepieion of Epidaurus
description: Most famous healing sanctuary of antiquity, with a theatre, tholos, and temple.

name: Asklepieion of Pergamon
description: Major healing centre, expanded in the Roman era. Associated with the physician Galen.

name: Asklepieion of Kos
description: Terraced healing sanctuary associated with Hippocrates on the island of Kos.

name: Sanctuary of the Great Gods (Samothrace)
description: Mystery cult centre where Philip II of Macedon met Olympias. Home of the Winged Victory.

name: Asklepieion of Athens
description: Healing sanctuary on the south slope of the Acropolis.

name: Nemesis Temple (Rhamnous)
description: Temple of the goddess of divine retribution at the Attic border fortress.

name: Temple of Aphaia (Aegina)
description: Well-preserved Doric temple on the island of Aegina with important pediment sculptures.

name: Temple of Apollo (Side)
description: Seaside temple at the Roman port city of Side in Pamphylia.

name: Temple of Trajan (Xanthos)
description: Imperial cult temple at the Lycian capital, one of three in the region.

name: Temple of Zeus (Euromos)
description: Well-preserved Corinthian temple in rural Caria with 16 standing columns.

name: Temple of Zeus (Aizanoi)
description: Best-preserved Roman temple in Anatolia with a subterranean chamber for the cult of Cybele.

name: Temple of Dionysus (Baalbek)
description: Small Roman temple at the Baalbek complex, near the Temple of Venus.

name: Temple of Eshmun (Sidon)
description: Phoenician healing-god sanctuary with Roman-era additions near Sidon.

name: Temple of Jupiter Heliopolitanus (Rome)
description: Temple of the Syrian Jupiter on the Janiculum Hill in Rome.

name: Sanctuary of Poseidon (Isthmia)
description: Panhellenic sanctuary hosting the Isthmian Games near Corinth.

name: Temple of Athena (Priene)
description: Ionic temple dedicated by Alexander the Great, paradigm of Ionic architecture.

name: Temple of Athena (Lindos)
description: Dramatic acropolis sanctuary on the island of Rhodes.

name: Temple of Apollo (Cyrene)
description: Major Greek temple at the Libyan colony, destroyed in the earthquake of 365 AD.

name: Capitol (Timgad)
description: Capitoline temple at the Trajanic colony of Thamugadi in Numidia.

name: Capitol (Lambaesis)
description: Temple at the legionary headquarters of Legio III Augusta in Numidia.

name: Temple of Juno Caelestis (Carthage)
description: Temple to the Romanized Tanit, closed by imperial decree in 421 AD.

name: Capitol (Cuicul/Djemila)
description: Capitoline temple in the well-preserved Roman town of Cuicul, Algeria.

name: Temple of Septimius Severus (Leptis Magna)
description: Imperial cult temple in the new forum built by the native-son emperor at Leptis Magna.

name: Temple of Liber Pater (Leptis Magna)
description: Temple of Dionysus/Liber at Leptis Magna, reflecting North African religious life.

name: Temple of Castor (Gigthis)
description: Temple at the southern Tunisian port of Gigthis.

name: Temple of Mercury (Thuburbo Majus)
description: Capitol and temples at the prosperous North African town.

name: Temple of Lenus Mars (Trier)
description: Major Gallo-Roman healing sanctuary combining Celtic Lenus with Roman Mars.

name: Temple of Mercury (Uley)
description: Romano-British temple with numerous curse tablets in the Cotswolds.

name: Temple of Nodens (Lydney)
description: Late Romano-British healing temple complex, studied by Tolkien who analysed the deity name.

name: Temple of Mithras (Capidava)
description: Mithraeum at the Danube frontier fort of Capidava in Moesia Inferior.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Viminacium)
description: Temple at the legionary fortress and provincial capital of Moesia Superior.

name: Temple of Diana (Nimes)
description: Ruined temple near the Jardins de la Fontaine in Nemausus, purpose debated.

name: Temple of Janus (Langres)
description: Gallo-Roman fanum at the civitas capital of the Lingones.

name: Sanctuary of Apollo Grannus (Grand)
description: Major healing sanctuary in the Vosges combining Celtic Grannus with Apollo, visited by Caracalla.

name: Temple of Mithras (Sarmizegetusa)
description: Mithraeum at the capital of Roman Dacia.

name: Temple of Mithras (Apulum)
description: Mithraeum at the legionary fortress of XIII Gemina in Dacia.

name: Temple of Mithras (Intercisa)
description: Mithraeum at the Danube fort garrisoned by Syrian archers.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Cumae)
description: Temple on the acropolis of Cumae, first Greek colony in mainland Italy.

name: Sibyl's Cave (Cumae)
description: Trapezoidal gallery at Cumae traditionally identified as the seat of the Cumaean Sibyl.

name: Temple of Apollo (Naxos)
description: Unfinished gateway (Portara) of a massive Archaic temple on the islet of Palatia.

name: Sanctuary of Demeter (Pergamon)
description: Terraced sanctuary for mystery rites on the slopes below the Pergamon acropolis.

name: Temple of Serapis (Pozzuoli)
description: Actually the macellum (market) of Puteoli, misidentified due to a Serapis statue found there.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Terracina)
description: Hilltop sanctuary at Tarracina overlooking the Tyrrhenian Sea and Via Appia.

name: Tychaion (Antioch)
description: Temple of the city goddess Tyche at Antioch, one of the four great cities of the Roman East.

name: Great Church of Antioch
description: Golden-roofed octagonal church built by Constantine, one of Christendom's most important early cathedrals.

name: Church of St. Mark (Alexandria)
description: Traditional seat of the Patriarch of Alexandria, founded by Mark the Evangelist.

name: Basilica of Sbeitla
description: Large Byzantine basilica with baptismal font at the North African town of Sufetula.

name: Sanctuary of Zeus (Nemea)
description: Panhellenic sanctuary hosting the Nemean Games, with a well-preserved Doric temple.

name: Temple of Athena Alea (Tegea)
description: Important Arcadian temple designed by Scopas, housed the tusks of the Calydonian Boar.

name: Temple of Roma and Augustus (Lepcis Magna)
description: Early imperial cult temple in the old forum of Leptis Magna.

name: Mithraeum of Syracuse
description: Rock-cut mithraeum in the catacombs area of Syracuse, Sicily.

name: Mithraeum of Merida
description: Mithraeum at Augusta Emerita, capital of Lusitania, with painted frescoes.

name: Mithraeum of Tarquinia
description: Mithraeum at the ancient Etruscan-turned-Roman city of Tarquinii.
